danger_signals.visual_form: Spike character (construction foreman) sprite on screen
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat to player; contact causes life loss
Hazard that destroys player on contact; life loss
Impassable obstruction; can trap player permanently if positioned poorly

danger_signals.visual_form: Walls, pillars, ladders that disappear when hammered
danger_signals.meaning: Risk of becoming trapped or unreachable; puzzle consequence

danger_signals.visual_form: Gap in platform structure after object destruction
danger_signals.meaning: Player can fall to bottom of playfield and lose positioning

safety_signals.meaning: Path to navigate vertical space; escape route from threats
Safe standing surface; foundation for movement

safety_signals.visual_form: Bomb sprite connected to destructible objects
safety_signals.meaning: Controlled destruction; can clear multiple threats simultaneously
Can redirect enemies harmlessly into background; temporary safety

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Levels designed with destructible puzzle elements; construction site aesthetic

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Antagonist actively knocking down objects and disrupting player
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appears reactively in levels to cause chaos; represents construction site management as threat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Destructible walls, pillars, or objects visible on grid
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies continue spawning; Mario can continue destroying objects

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: All target destructible objects removed from visible grid
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Level progression allowed; transition to next stage

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mario surrounded by indestructible barrels or unreachable area
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Level must be aborted; game reset mechanic triggered

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Invisible grid structure; positioning of destructible vs indestructible objects
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must plan destruction order; suboptimal destruction prevents level completion

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Bonus items appear on screen after chain destruction
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Extra points awarded for multi-object destruction chains

environmental_storytelling.example: Playfield filled with destructible walls, pillars, ladders, barrels—visual language communicates industrial work environment and demolition theme
Mario cannot jump due to hammer weight; large hammer sprite defines gameplay constraint and world logic (realism of construction labor)
Invisible grid organizing all objects; conveys systematic demolition task and puzzle-design approach to destruction
Spike (Blackie) as construction foreman actively knocking down objects; visual character role communicates workplace chaos and management conflict
Destroyed objects do not respawn; visual absence of object communicates permanent state change and puzzle irreversibility

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Walks horizontally, climbs ladders, swings hammer to destroy objects; cannot jump
Avoided by player control; contact results in life loss and respawn
Chases Mario; knocks down destructible objects to disrupt player progress and create falling hazards
Redirected harmlessly into background when door is opened; becomes non-threatening
Destroy all connected destructible objects simultaneously; visual cascade of destruction
Appear in level to increase difficulty; patrol or chase based on proximity to Mario

notes: Wrecking Crew uses minimal visual factions (Player vs. Antagonist/Spike) with world-state communicated primarily through destructible object presence/absence and spatial puzzle configuration. The game's 'danger' is primarily puzzle-based (becoming trapped) rather than combat-heavy. The construction site aesthetic and tool-based mechanic (hammer weight preventing jumping) create environmental storytelling through gameplay constraint. No formal political or faction systems present; conflict is worker vs. foreman antagonism.
